title: Deep Cleaning America's Political House
source: The Guardian - The Nation – July - August 2026
content:
It is as though our great Declaration and Constitution represent a great political house with empty rooms. There have been empty promises and empty dreams for so many. While we believe rights are endowed by God and thus inalienable, we have only been partially successful in filling this house over the past 250 years. We have also seen repeated efforts to remove progress and reject what we thought was permanently established in America. As a theologian and student of Scripture, I believe our nation needs a message of profound renewal.

In the New Testament, Luke records Jesus saying that when an impure spirit leaves a person and finds no rest, it returns with seven more wicked spirits if the house is merely swept but not occupied with good. Similarly, our national house requires more than just a surface sweep. We must embark on a deep cleaning by building a coalition to remove corrupt political leaders and extremists who have abused their power. We need to demand fully funded public education, affordable housing, green jobs, and justice for all.

My grandmother used to clean her house with a handmade straw broom before she could finally afford a real one. Whenever she was able to buy a new piece of furniture, she insisted on doing a deep cleaning before bringing it inside. This must become the new rule in our national house: before we bring in the new, we must perform a thorough and lasting deep clean. This is no time for partial measures or simply going along to get along.
